description:
Your CI / CD pipeline for mobile apps uses a farm of build agents.  
  At the end of every run a master node writes a health file called  
  /home/user/build_logs/agent_status.log.

  Current file format (one single-line record per agent, no spaces except the
  one shown between the three key/value pairs, keys are always lower-case):

      agent_id:<ID> status:<OK|FAIL> latency_ms:<POSITIVE_INTEGER>

  Example:

      agent_id:A01 status:OK latency_ms:94

  GOAL  
  ----  
  1. Read /home/user/build_logs/agent_status.log.  
  2. Detect every agent whose status field is exactly “FAIL”.  
  3. Create the directory /home/user/build_reports (if it does not yet exist).  
  4. Write a plain-text report file named  
     /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt that contains **only** the
     alphanumeric agent IDs of the failed agents, one per line, sorted in
     ascending (lexicographical) order.  No blank lines, no extra text.
  5. When finished, print the absolute path of the generated
     /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt file to STDOUT so the next
     automation step can pick it up.

  Output Format that the grader will check
  ----------------------------------------
  • The file /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt must exist.  
  • Its exact contents must match the following regular expression  
    (POSIX extended):  

        ^([A-Za-z0-9]+(\n|$))+$

    In other words, one or more non-empty lines, each line containing only the
    agent ID with no surrounding whitespace, and a trailing newline on the last
    line is optional.  
  • The IDs must be sorted lexicographically (e.g., A01 before A12).  
  • Your terminal should finally echo the full path  
    /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt as the last line printed.

  Hints  
  -----  
  • Typical Unix text-processing utilities (grep, awk, cut, sort, etc.) are
    sufficient; expect to use only two or three commands total.  
  • You do not need root privileges; everything lives in /home/user.  
  • Avoid hard-coding the failing agent IDs; determine them from the log file.

  When you are done you can run “cat /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt”
  to visually verify your result, but remember the grader will use automated
  checks based on the rules above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

  1. Directory /home/user/build_logs exists (0755 user:user).  
  2. File /home/user/build_logs/agent_status.log exists (0644 user:user) with
     the following exact contents (one trailing newline after the last line):

        agent_id:A01 status:OK latency_ms:94
        agent_id:A12 status:FAIL latency_ms:256
        agent_id:B03 status:OK latency_ms:101
        agent_id:C07 status:FAIL latency_ms:305
        agent_id:D99 status:OK latency_ms:87

  3. Directory /home/user/build_reports does NOT exist.

  Expected state after successful completion:

  1. Directory /home/user/build_reports exists (0755 user:user).  
  2. File /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt exists (0644 user:user)
     with the following exact contents (including a final newline):

        A12
        C07

  The agent’s final line to STDOUT must be exactly:

      /home/user/build_reports/failure_summary.txt
name: task_000000_e0b1b4b9
